_phase_tt_note: Phase TT.0 failure-classifier rules. Hot-reloadable. The Swift binary at WizardFailureClassifier.swift knows the SHAPE of categories (.needsDecomposition / .realBug / .promptMiss / .qualityFail / .ok); this file holds the CONTENT (thresholds + pattern strings). Updating these rules is a content PR — no rebuild required, picked up on next refreshMchataisourceCache.

thresholds._note_minimal: Below this, output is judged 'no real work happened'. Tuned from the 32-second tap-target failure (≈600 bytes of conversational filler).
thresholds._note_substantial: Above this, output suggests the wizard genuinely produced 60-80% of an artifact. Tuned from the 600s timeouts that emitted 30-50KB of partial code before giving up.

promptMissPatterns._note: Output prefixes that indicate the wizard didn't recognize this as a build request. Each is a substring match against the first ~500 chars of output. Add new patterns when you observe a new way the wizard's planner can derail into chat-mode.
patterns: Before I plan this — a couple quick design
What kind of thing are you looking to do?

realBugPatterns._note: Output prefixes that indicate wizard infrastructure failure (model error, parser failure, tunnel wedge). Decomposition won't help — these need human or agent attention.
